title: Each thought loop: a thread you set down
subtitle: An energy statement for practising non-engagement with recurring thoughts.
excerpt: A short energy statement from Day 14 of the SLEEP program. The image reframes thought loops as threads: something you pick up and can also set down, rather than something that runs automatically until it exhausts itself.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 14 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

practice.whenToUse: Use in the morning to set the intention for the day, or in any moment when you catch a thought loop beginning. One round, said with full attention.

content.text: A short energy statement from Day 14 of the SLEEP program. The image is a practical one: a thought loop is a thread. You pick it up. You can also set it down. This statement makes that gesture explicit and repeatable.
Sit comfortably. Take one slow breath and then say:
I notice this thought. I don't need to follow it. I set it down.

Say it three times in the morning, imagining each repetition as a practice run for the moment the loop actually arrives. Then use it again in real time: the thought appears, you notice it, you say the statement once, you let it pass. You are not suppressing the thought — you are changing your relationship to it.
Adapted from Day 14 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
